items.title: Bondscoach Portugal: 'Ronaldo helemaal niet bezig met duizendste doelpunt'
items.summary: Roberto Martínez ziet een mentaliteitsverandering bij Cristiano Ronaldo. Waar de Portugese vedette voorheen gefixeerd was op het maken van doelpunten, is de 41-jarige aanvaller volgens de bondscoach van Portugal meer en meer een teamspeler geworden. Dat bleek volgens Martínez al tijdens het EK. De Spaanse trainer stelt dan ook dat het maken van zijn duizendste goal geen doel op zich is voor Ronaldo, terwijl dit juist een belangrijke bron van motivatie leek voor de sterspeler van Al-Nassr om zo lang door te gaan.

items.title: Champions League draw, Premier League news, and more: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend ⚽ Champions League draw from 11am (GMT) | Mail Barry Liverpool: We’ll kick off with some bad news for Liverpool fans, ahead of their side’s match against West Ham tomorrow. Arne Slot has opened his pre-match presser by telling reporters that Florian Wirtz will not be fit for the game and there’s no confirmed time frame on the German’s return. Wirtz missed Liverpool’s somewhat fortuitous win against Nottingham Forest last weekend after being forced to pull out during the warm-up. “I don’t expect Florian to be available,” he says. “It’s a disappointment. Every time a player is not available is a disappointment, it’s something that I didn’t expect after last week. At this moment time, I don’t know [when he will return]. Usually when I say that, I do know and I don’t want to share. But this time, I honestly don’t know.” Slot also reveals that Jeremie Frimpong will be available for tomorrow’s match after being sidelined since the end of January. Continue reading...

items.title: Slot heeft goed nieuws over Frimpong, maar baalt van tegenslag Wirtz
items.summary: Jeremie Frimpong (25) keert dit weekend terug in de wedstrijdselectie van Arne Slot. De vleugelspeler van het Nederlands elftal stond een aantal weken aan de kant met een blessure. Zaterdag maakt de Oranje-international echter zijn rentree voor Liverpool.

items.title: Rondje Europa: AZ redt Nederlandse eer en strijdt op drie fronten
items.summary: AZ redt voorlopig de Nederlandse eer in Europa. De Alkmaarders bogen een achterstand om in de return tegen FC Noah, waardoor er een ticket voor de achtste finales van de Conference League veilig is gesteld. Met clubwatcher Simon Zwartkruis en verslaggever Geert-Jan Jakobs blikken we terug op de avond.

items.title: Schrik in Puerto Rico: Messi gevloerd door veldbestormer
items.summary: Lionel Messi werd donderdagavond belaagd tijdens een oefenwedstrijd in Puerto Rico. Diverse fans renden het veld op voor een moment met de wereldkampioen van Inter Miami FC, maar één van hen maakte het wel erg bont. Daardoor ging Messi naar de grond en was er even grote schrik.

items.title: Ruben Amorim sacking will cost Manchester United up to £15.9m
items.summary: Cost of Ten Hag exit then having Amorim may hit £36.3m Settlement for Amorim and his staff revealed in filing Manchester United sacking Ruben Amorim could end up costing the club almost £16m. Amorim’s 14-month reign ended on 5 January after his public attack on United’s hierarchy, with his five coaches also leaving Old Trafford. A filing to the New York Stock Exchange revealed the potential payments to Amorim and his staff, a day after the club confirmed they had made a £32.6m profit in their second-quarter results to 31 December 2025. Continue reading...

items.title: Nieuwe plannen tegen tijdrekken: ingooi kost tien procent van een wedstrijd
items.summary: De internationale spelregelcommissie IFAB komt zaterdagochtend in Wales bijeen voor de jaarlijkse vergadering over de nieuwe regels voor het seizoen 2026/27. Bovenaan de agenda staan maatregelen om het tempo in wedstrijden op te voeren. De kans is groot dat er een vijfsecondenregel wordt ingevoerd bij inworpen en doeltrappen, om zo de zuivere speeltijd te vergroten.

items.title: Rhodes stays in hunt, two off lead at Women's World Championship
items.summary: England's Mimi Rhodes remains two shots off leader Auston Kim after the second round of the HSBC Women's World Championship in Singapore.

items.title: What could happen in Champions League round-of-16 draw?
items.summary: The draw for the Champions League round of 16 is set for Friday at 6 a.m. ET (11 a.m. GMT) but how does it work and which teams are likely to face each other?

items.title: Forest dodge fireworks before advancing in UEL
items.summary: Nottingham Forest players were forced to dodge fireworks thrown on to the pitch by Fenerbahce fans at the start of their Europa League playoff second leg.

items.title: Messi scores as Miami wins rescheduled friendly
items.summary: Inter Miami CF beat Independiente del Valle 2-1 on Thursday night in Puerto Rico with goals from Santiago Morales and Lionel Messi to conclude the team's preseason friendly tour.

items.title: Klopp's job at risk? 'Nonsense,' says Red Bull
items.summary: Red Bull backed former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp to remain the drinks giant's head of global soccer and dismissed a media report that his job could be under threat after barely a year.

items.title: Neymar shushes critics after first goals in return
items.summary: Neymar broke his 2026 goal drought in spectacular fashion Thursday, leading Santos to a 2-1 victory over Vasco da Gama in the fourth round of the Brasileirão.

items.title: Will Aston Villa hold on to their place in the Champions League?
items.summary: Villa’s run of three wins in nine league games has opened the door to Manchester United, Chelsea and Liverpool By WhoScored Two months ago, Aston Villa were just three points off top spot in the Premier League. They were on a run of 12 wins in 14 games that included victories against Manchester City and Arsenal. Their run of eight consecutive wins in the league was their best since they won 10 in a row in 1910. You wouldn’t have blamed Villa fans for daring to dream about lifting their first league title since 1981. But success breeds expectation, and expectation carries its own cruel weight. Arsenal tore Villa apart in a 4-1 win in late December and victories have been hard to come by in the new year – they have only won three of their last nine league games. Liverpool, Manchester United and Chelsea are still below them in the table but the gap is narrowing. With games against United and Chelsea coming in March, the top-three spot Villa have occupied since they beat Wolves in late November suddenly looks in jeopardy. So does their place in the Champions League next season. Continue reading...

items.title: From Hearts failure to Estoril excitement - the 'most Portuguese Scot there is'
items.summary: After a forgettable seven months in charge at Hearts in 2017, Scottish manager Ian Cathro is rebuilding his reputation at Estoril.

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Ferrous and El Bodon clash in Lingfield Sprint Trial
items.summary: We have a busy day's racing on Friday with action from Doncaster, Lingfield, Newbury and Wolverhampton, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: Brundle: Russell 'ready' for first F1 title challenge
items.summary: George Russell is "ready" to fight for a first F1 world championship in the 2026 season, says Sky Sports F1's Martin Brundle.

items.title: Warren: Dubois' pre-fight party 'can't happen again'
items.summary: The pre-fight ‘party’ that took place ahead of Daniel Dubois’ defeat to Oleksandr Usyk “can’t happen again”, says promoter Frank Warren.

items.title: 'Don't know what fuss was about' - chaotic Palace season could end in glory
items.summary: Key players have gone, the manager is leaving and has criticised the club and upset fans - yet Crystal Palace's chaotic campaign could still end in European glory.

items.title: Sacking Amorim could cost Man Utd nearly £16m
items.summary: Ruben Amorim's 14 months in charge of Manchester United have come at a price, with the club now paying up to £15.9m to sack him, in addition to £11m to hire the Portuguese.
